title: Exercise Red Flag-Alaska Launches Multinational Air Combat Training
description: Exercise Red Flag-Alaska 26-2 brought together more than 2,100 service members from the U.S. and partner nations for realistic air combat training at Eielson Air Force Base and Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Alaska.

body.text: Exercise Red Flag-Alaska 26-2 commenced May 28, bringing together more than 2,100 service members from the U.S. and partner nations for realistic air combat training at Eielson Air Force Base and Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Alaska.

Army paratroopers assigned to the 2nd Infantry Brigade Combat Team, 11th Airborne Division, conduct prejump rigging inspections prior to an airborne operation during Exercise Red Flag-Alaska 26-2 at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Alaska, June 1, 2026. The 36th Airlift Squadron provided tactical airlift support to the airborne division, enabling airborne operations in a realistic combat environment.
